programs.name: Charity Care for Low-Income Seniors
programs.what_it_does: Provides free or discounted healthcare services including hospital charges, doctor's visits, medications, and X-rays. Available at any time, even for previously paid bills or those in collections.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors within 300% of the federal poverty level.[1]
programs.whats_unique: Newly passed state law expanding charity care specifically highlighted for WA seniors, offering broad coverage beyond standard federal programs.[1]
programs.benefit_value: Free or half-price on covered services; exact value varies by bill amount.[1]

programs.name: Housing and Essential Needs (HEN) Referral Program
programs.what_it_does: Offers financial assistance for essential needs like rent, utilities, transportation, home repairs, food, and personal health items. Helps reduce overall care costs including medical bills indirectly.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors and families meeting income and disability requirements.[1]
programs.whats_unique: State-specific referral program covering broad daily essentials tailored to WA's low-income seniors and disabled.[1]
programs.benefit_value: Varies by need; covers specific essential expenses.[1]

programs.name: Aged, Blind, or Disabled (ABD) Cash Assistance Program
programs.what_it_does: Provides cash to cover monthly bills, food, and housing essentials. Supplements Social Security for those unable to work due to health reasons, aiding medical costs.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors aged 65+ who are aged, blind, or disabled and unable to work.[1]
programs.whats_unique: WA's state cash assistance program specifically for ABD seniors, beyond federal SSI supplements.[1][2]
programs.benefit_value: Monthly cash payments; amount based on income and needs (specific figures not detailed).[1]

programs.name: Tailored Supports for Older Adults (TSOA)
programs.what_it_does: Pays for caregiver support services like counseling, training, medical equipment, and respite care. Also funds personal assistance if no caregiver is available.[2]
programs.who_its_for: Older adults needing support for unpaid family caregivers or personal assistance.[2]
programs.whats_unique: WA-specific program under Long-Term Services, focusing on tailored caregiver and personal supports not standard federally.[2]
programs.benefit_value: Covers specified services; value depends on approved needs.[2]

programs.name: WA Cares Fund
programs.what_it_does: Provides access to long-term care services and supports for working Washingtonians. Allows flexible choice of covered services to meet individual care needs.[6]
programs.who_its_for: Working Washingtonians earning access to long-term care benefits.[6]
programs.whats_unique: State-funded long-term care insurance program unique to WA, mandatory payroll deduction for comprehensive LTC coverage.[6]
programs.benefit_value: Up to $36,500 lifetime benefit (as of prior knowledge; confirm current); flexible service combinations.[6]
